programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve gets strangled by a python on a night-time river expedition, climbs a 40-metre tree and finds out why our thirst for palm oil is threatening bears and orangutans.

programme.title: Despicable Me
programme.desc: Delighting in all things wicked, supervillain Gru hatches a plot to steal the moon. Surrounded by an army of little yellow Minions, he has to incorporate three orphans into his evil plans.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: After an island-wide blackout causes havoc in Honoré, the team trace the source to a local substation and discover an electrocuted dead body. On first appearances, it seems like a tragic accident, but when Neville and the team investigate the computer repair shop where the victim worked, they discover a hidden world of data mining and crypto currency. It turns out the victim was stealing electricity in order to mine thousands and thousands in crypto. With so much money at stake, it soon becomes clear that this was no accidental death after all. The only problem is that all three suspects were together at the time of the victim’s electrocution. So if it was murder, how did they do it?

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Disarming Welsh DS Janie Mallowan is desperate to solve some big cases and progress her career. While investigating the death by apparent suicide of wealthy property developer Carwyn Rees, she meets none other than John Chapel, the retired actor who played her TV detective idol, Inspector Charles Caesar. Eccentric and reclusive John uses his insight into character to deduce that Carwyn was murdered, though Janie is initially sceptical of his conclusions. When forensics prove his theory right, Janie quietly asks him to help with the case. Having shut himself away from the world for years, John is initially reluctant to help. But together they work to solve an intriguing mystery involving an environmental activist, a grieving widow and a bitter business rival, painting the portrait of a man who kept a lot of secrets from those closest to him. And although John and Janie are not ready to admit it, this first case is the start of an iconic crime-solving partnership, and a friendship, too.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: After a walker is found dead on a Welsh mountain trail, John’s insight into the unidentified woman quickly turns this into a murder investigation. Janie ropes John into joining her mam Yvonne’s walking group to help solve the case and crack the identity of this mysterious woman. John is reluctant to go out and socialise, but he can’t resist the call of another mystery. John and Janie join forces to solve two mysteries: the identity of the victim and a cunning murder plot with a large dose of blackmail and revenge. Janie’s fierce defence of one of the suspects reveals more about her past, while John finds himself out of the house and enjoying himself a lot more than he’s ready to admit...

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Dickie's friends and family gather round when his boyfriend dumps him on the doorstep. Can he fight for his love and win back his man?

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: Sick of dating creeps, Lucinda is given the royal princess treatment courtesy of her fairy godmother. Sal auditions for a talent show, and Dickie offers sage advice in the workplace.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast, from Northern Irish creator rarewitch. The cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical, and Gambo forgets how to breathe. Surreal, chaotic and hilariously absurd – welcome to the madcap world of Glue.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The Crown Prosecution Service has decided to take the women’s rape cases to trial. But with court delays and barrister strikes, will they ever get their day in court?

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Linda Walsh, Conor's one true love, is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Ian and the team deal with a newly published report from the United Nations health agency which concludes that some key host cities may now be too hot to safely play football in due to global heating. The challenge is how to get ahead of the narrative so that the scheduled matches can still take place. Meanwhile, Will is settling in to life at oversight, and the social media team figure that the only way to get a younger US audience interested in the tournament is to put a vibe under the whole idea of football by getting a major celebrity involved, if they can only find one.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: It’s the final, and after 12 tough weeks, the last two candidates standing must go head-to-head in a battle to win Lord Sugar’s investment. Supported by returning candidates, the finalists must launch their businesses to a roomful of industry experts, as well as some legends from the last 20 years of the show. In the boardroom, Lord Sugar decides who will become his next business partner.

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: The Apprentice has a winner! Angela Scanlon hears from Lord Sugar and his newest business partner. As well as talking to the runner up, she is also joined by Baroness Brady and Tim Campbell.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel meets an old friend in County Durham who appears determined not to learn from his previous mistakes. Martin is pleased with the area he’s in, just maybe not the property. Tommy thinks a demolition derby might be in order in Kent.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley takes the helm as retirees Anthea and Keith look to upsize from their one-bedroom terraced cottage in a Hertfordshire town to a three-bedroom detached home in the Worcestershire countryside. Despite having viewed 16 properties, the couple have yet to find ‘the one’. Ginny presents them with several contenders, including a lovingly refurbished timber-framed Grade II listed cottage, a 125-year-old property with an exquisite country garden, and a Victorian home that maxes out on views. Exploring the magical Malvern Hills, Ginny also meets a family who quit the London rat race to establish an award-winning brewery.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s finals week, where the winners from the heats compete to cook a course at a red-carpet banquet in Liverpool’s St George’s Hall to celebrate the British film industry. The eight rivals from all corners of the UK compete over a different course, from starter through to dessert, where they will be marked out of ten by an expert judging panel, including a surprise guest judge - in this episode, film director Marli Siu. It’s a nerve-wracking first day as the chefs settle into the kitchen and meet their rivals. Each wants to win a course at the banquet and get their name on the menu. Can they improve on their scores from the first rounds?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 5 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Keir Starmer takes PMQs as the row over the vetting of Peter Mandelson for US ambassador continues.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 5 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: One of Japan’s cultural highlights is the tea ceremony. Michael visits a Kyushu tea farm where morning fog shades the tea plants, helping the leaves grow greener and richer in flavour. Michael takes one of the newest Shinkansen lines to Nagasaki, the only port open to foreign trade when Japan was closed to the outside world for more than 200 years. Michael learns of the Scottish industrialist Thomas Glover, who helped samurai clans overthrow the shogun and played a pivotal role in Japan’s industrialisation. There is also a boat trip to visit a remarkable coal-mining island, first developed in the 1890s, which grew into a small town with schools, barbers and a cinema. During the 1960s, it had a population density nine times that of central Tokyo.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Eight regional champions cook their main courses again in a bid to secure a place at a banquet celebrating the British film industry. The multi-award-winning director of such films as The Queen, Philomena, My Beautiful Laundrette and Dangerous Liaisons, Sir Stephen Frears, joins Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight main courses. And in a twist, a mystery wildcard chef is invited into the kitchen to enter the fray. Can they steal the winning crown from the other chefs?

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores some of this year's most exciting archaeological finds from the east of Britain. Each discovery comes straight from the trenches/site, filmed by the archaeologists themselves. We unearth the biggest collection of Roman writing tablets in Britain, giving insight into what Roman London was really like. Off the coast of Kent, we dive into the English Channel to complete the biggest marine excavation since the Mary Rose - an 18th-century East India Company ship, packed with silver. Also in Kent, we're on the detective trail to find the very first evidence of Julius Caesar's invasion of Britain - an ancient fort scattered with human skulls and weapons.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: Following Jackson from the shockwaves of the Martin Bashir documentary Living with Michael Jackson to the final act of his life - and the battle over what he leaves behind. In the aftermath of the documentary, insiders describe a man in free-fall: frightened, financially exposed and increasingly isolated. Manager Dieter Wiesner recalls Michael rallying his fanbase, while rumours of mounting debts and allegations escalate. When 12-year-old Gavin Arvizo goes to police, prosecutor Ron Zonen asserts Jackson knew it was 'an inappropriate relationship'. The singer is arrested and charged, and becomes the centre of a trial that consumes a small town and the global media.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: It’s another morning at oversight HQ, and the team have scheduled a virtual meeting with David Beckham to finalise arrangements for the announcement of the tournament ambassador. But it’s possible that Zurich has other ideas. Sarah is to go on a Gen Z podcast that has criticised the tournament’s carbon output. The social media team take a new campaign idea out onto the streets, and Will masters the coffee machine. When the time comes for the ambassador announcement, the team suddenly find themselves with two in the room at the same time and no plan.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Faisal Islam.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: This week John Simpson, in discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, analyses the confrontation between Iran and the United States in the Gulf, looks at the humanitarian situation in Lebanon and examines the effect of the downfall of the Hungarian Prime Minister, Viktor Orban, on the European Union.

programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks
programme.desc: Steeped in myth and untamed beauty, Dartmoor National Park's vast moorlands, characterised by open heathland, grassy hills and striking granite tors, provide a a sanctuary for rare plant and animal species as well as a treasure trove for archaeologists. Rivers meandering through the moors are home to salmon, otters and kingfishers, and peatlands host rare plants like the carnivorous sundew.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from day five of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre.

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: When a seaweed farm is targeted in an act of sabotage, all the evidence points to the legendary mermaid of Shipton Abbott.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Actor Will Poulter, who studied chef skills for his role in The Bear, joins Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight fish courses, which are all inspired by the British film industry. With Tom remarking that every single piece of fish is cooked perfectly, the judges have their work cut out in picking a winner, and the decision goes right to the wire. Only one of the chefs will earn the right to cook their fish course at the banquet.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims continue their adventure in Durham and visit the 12th-century Norman cathedral. They are met by Canon Charlie Allen, who introduces them to the ancient pilgrim tradition of foot washing, although not all the pilgrims are keen to proffer their naked feet. Leaving Durham behind them, they head for Heavenfield, an ancient battlefield, and Housesteads, the UK’s best-preserved Roman fort on Hadrian’s Wall. Some of them head to Newcastle to visit the city's Reform synagogue. For another taste of hermit life, the pilgrims follow St Oswald’s Way towards a hermitage from the early 1400s. Belonging to Warkworth Castle, the hermitage is carved deep inside a rock. It has its own tiny chapel and is where a hermit priest once lived. Along the way, the pilgrims start to open up and reveal more of themselves as the journey continues.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel meets a man in Carnoustie renovating a home for him, his dog and a menagerie of other animals to live in. Dion decides it’s not safe for him to go into a house in Merseyside. Jacqui is in Essex, where there’s space to play with.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from a busy seaside resort dream of a new life in Somerset for their young family, with outside space for chickens, llamas and maybe even a pig. Denise Nurse takes on their countryside search, lining up a lovely selection of homes, including one with spectacular views across the Bristol Channel to Wales. She also meets a creative pair who have already made their escape to the country and gets hands on with some screen printing.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In a special programme, Monty gets an exclusive preview of a brand new garden created in Regent’s Park, London, to commemorate the life of Queen Elizabeth II, due to open on what would have been her 100th birthday. Over the last two years, a disused plant nursery has been transformed into a visionary garden, open to all. A vibrant, living tribute filled with wildlife, sustainable planting and a bold design for the future. Meanwhile, the team have been behind the scenes, watching the garden come to life from its very first foundations. Advolly Richmond uncovered the park’s extraordinary past, from its origins as a Tudor royal hunting ground to its reinvention as wartime allotments. And on a cold winter’s day, Adam Frost rolled up his sleeves to help volunteers plant 11,000 spring bulbs. Frances Tophill explored the magnolia, a tree much admired for its grace and resilience - qualities often associated with the late Queen. And Carol Klein gained access to a hidden gem of a nursery tasked with growing more than a quarter of a million plants for the Royal Parks, and uncovers the secrets of growing pelargoniums fit for a king.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 6 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The Flog It! team visit Herstmonceux Castle, where antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and James Lewis find treasures to take to auction, including some beautiful bronze sculptures. Paul Martin takes a trip on a very large miniature railway.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael arrives in the home of Japanese porcelain, Arita, where a railway line runs straight through the town’s stunning ceramic Shinto shrine. He learns about the traditional blue underglaze, gosu, and turns his hand to pottery decoration. Japan’s volcanic geology means the country is full of thermal spa resorts. Michael stays in a ryokan, a traditional hotel with paper walls and tatami mats where he enjoys a relaxing soak in an onsen, a natural thermal bath. Michael ends this leg in Fukuoka, Japan’s fast-growing city, full of young Japanese who flock there for the food culture and entrepreneurial spirit. At one of the many street food stalls, Michael meets the mayor, an ex-TV presenter turned politician who is transforming the city.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s the dessert final, the last chance for the chefs to win a dish at a red-carpet banquet in Liverpool to celebrate the British film industry. Michelin-starred chefs Tom Kerridge and Lorna McNee and comedian Phil Wang join GBM royalty Dame Prue Leith on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight fantastic dessert courses.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The Williamsons go for glory at their local show on Lewis, where their best sheep compete with the island's cream of the crop. Lucy and Adam reignite their dairy dreams, but their new baby arrives just as the workload ramps up. And Callum and Zara head south for a farm safety awareness event to commemorate those farmers and their families affected by accidents at work.

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Whenever their pagers sound, the volunteers of the RNLI immediately find themselves under pressure. In Port Isaac on the north coast of Cornwall, the crew race to the rescue of a woman with a spinal injury; she’s in a precarious position on a rock face, with the light fading. In Pembrokeshire, south west Wales, the Little & Broadhaven crew receive a mayday call about a capsized dinghy with three people in the water. In Mudeford on the Dorset coast, a search for a missing kayaker quickly escalates when it emerges that there are six more kayaks unaccounted for. And in Eyemouth on the east coast of Scotland, the crew respond to an unusual rescue: a swan that’s become tangled in fishing line in the harbour.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul, Bob and Ted travel to one of the UK’s most northerly chalk streams, Driffield Beck at Mulberry Whin in East Riding of Yorkshire. The river has a constant temperature of 10 degrees Celsius, which means the environment is like a rainforest, with year-round stable conditions that attract a range of wildlife. It’s an idyllic setting to fish, this time for wild brown trout. They waste no time getting started but are slightly distracted by Ted, who is obsessed with rolling in the mud on the riverbank, getting himself into a very messy state - leading to Bob booking an appointment at a luxurious doggy spa not far from their fishing spot.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell.

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh travels to Rwanda to discover its unique culture, experience incredible wildlife and find out how the country has healed and rebuilt itself after the 1994 genocide.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from the sixth day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Eight regional champions cook their main courses again in a bid to secure a place at a banquet celebrating the British film industry. The multi-award-winning director of such films as The Queen, Philomena, My Beautiful Laundrette and Dangerous Liaisons, Sir Stephen Frears, joins Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight main courses. And in a twist, a mystery wildcard chef is invited into the kitchen to enter the fray. Can they steal the winning crown from the other chefs?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s the dessert final, the last chance for the chefs to win a dish at a red-carpet banquet in Liverpool to celebrate the British film industry. Michelin-starred chefs Tom Kerridge and Lorna McNee and comedian Phil Wang join GBM royalty Dame Prue Leith on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight fantastic dessert courses.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: In August 1991, Michael signs the most lucrative deal in music history with Sony Music and prepares for what his team believes will be a landmark year. Then, a 13-year-old boy accuses Michael of child sexual abuse. Detectives describe the shock of investigating the world’s most famous performer, while inside Michael’s camp, aides recall disbelief and a frantic effort to keep Michael’s worldwide tour, business empire and public image intact. As the story breaks, police search Michael’s home, and the media storm intensifies. Michael publicly denies the allegations, condemning what he calls a 'trial by media'.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: After coming to Dom’s business boot camp in the summer of 2023, stone carver Louise from Oxfordshire and stained-glass artist Charlotte from Bristol built dream businesses. Louise arrived at the boot camp, looking for help to make stone carving her full-time job. Now, Dom visits her studio to find out how she has done exactly that and how much money she makes from it. Charlotte reveals how she has built a sustainable stained-glass business after giving up her job in a vet’s practice and how much she now earns from her work.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion time travels back to the 70s in Nottinghamshire, and it all feels very familiar. Martin is loving his surroundings in Bath, and Jacqui sees beyond the work that needs done in Norfolk to appreciate the potential.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay, but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it’s Ronan’s next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling – the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: In the ultimate knockout quiz, eight players do battle on the Tournament Run in a series of exciting tug-of-war quiz matches as they attempt to knock their opponents out of the game and steal their cash. Only one player will be left standing at the end, but can they make it all the way to the Golden Run to double their money? Hosted by Alex Scott.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael leaves Kyushu by foot tunnel under the Kanmon straits to arrive on the main island of Honshu at the harbour town of Shimonoseki. Here, he encounters the deadly delicacy of pufferfish. Containing a neurotoxin with no known antidote, only highly trained chefs can prepare the fish. Will Michael give it a try? Travelling eastwards along the Honshu coast, Michael visits Hitachi’s Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured. He is thrilled to get inside the factory and see these amazing machines up close. To end this leg, Michael arrives by Shinkansen into Hiroshima, the city devastated by the atomic bomb dropped by US forces on 6th August 1945. Michael crosses the city to visit the Peace Memorial Park, where he meets Keiko Ogura, a bomb survivor who was just eight years old on that fateful day.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search, with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster, to find Northern Ireland’s greatest garden. The judges face a difficult decision when it comes to choosing. Ivydene garden near Comber is spread across three acres. Owners Robert and Michael have three separate areas of planting - a formal layout at the front of their home, a secret garden at the side, and at the back, a wildflower meadow and woodland area. In total contrast, there’s a tropical backyard over in Lisburn, where owner George has sourced unusual plants from all around the world. Finally, the judges view a rock garden in Ballynahinch. A large expanse of native rock dominates this four-acre garden, created by passionate owner Brigid. Diarmuid, Carol and Penny are searching for a garden which the owners have poured their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide is worthy of a place in the Greatest Garden final?

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There is a lot of productive gardening going on at Beechgrove as George Anderson and Ruth Vichos both work on projects that will bear fruit later in the year. Ruth is focusing on growing for those with a small space, returning to the vertical garden that she started last year. Vertical gardening uses structures that allow plants to be stacked upwards - ideal for anyone with just a small plot or patio. She also shows how this method can still be very productive. George is in his beloved fruit cage, where after a clear-out last year, he plants a bed of new raspberry varieties and propagates new growth from Beechgrove’s existing fig tree. There is a Back to Basics guide from Colin Crosbie’s plot just outside Dumfries, and a review of Beechgrove’s gravel and grass garden, which will be thinned and replanted over the course of this season.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the garden explodes with spring colour, Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. He also plants in the woodland garden and pots up summer bulbs. Sue Kent is in her vibrant Swansea garden, sowing tender crops, planting echiums and harvesting a delicious salad bowl. Jamie Butterworth is in Essex to discover why the humble euphorbia deserves to be considered one of the brightest stars of the border. There’s also a trip to West Sussex to visit renowned garden designer Rosemary Alexander in her private garden. Plus a passionate anemone grower preparing for their debut at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s the banquet finale, where the overall winner of this 20th anniversary competition will be decided at a red-carpet event at St George’s Hall, Liverpool. The judges, Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang, have chosen the chefs from different regions of the country to create a special banquet. It's being served to celebrate the great and the good of the British film industry, including BAFTA and Oscar winners, actors, directors, costume designers, casting directors and more. The chefs need to work together to wow the guests with their depth of flavour and flawless presentation. Who will triumph and become 2026's champion of champions?

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Kerry Godliman introduces Suzi Ruffell and Loyiso Gola to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: Don't Look Now
programme.desc: Grieving the loss of their young daughter in a tragic accident, Laura and John Baxter visit Venice, where John is helping to restore a dilapidated church. As John works, Laura is befriended by two sisters, one of whom warns them to leave the city, claiming she has seen their dead daughter. Classic mystery thriller from Nicolas Roeg.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship

programme.title: Azaad Desh Ke Gulam
programme.desc: Bharti, a law student from a rich family, lives a comfortable life. Her life changes when she learns that her father is in illegal activities.

programme.title: Pati Patni Aur Woh 1978
programme.desc: Adam & Eve live a worry-free life in paradise. They are instructed not to eat an apple. One day they cannot resist the temptation, and both take a bite out of it. Nature takes offense and expels them from heaven.

programme.title: Choron Ki Baarat
programme.desc: Anju, a beautiful and spoilt woman, falls in love with a mysterious man, Shekhar. However, with time she realises that he has other motives.

programme.title: Khote Sikkay
programme.desc: An unknown horseback rider is after Jhanga, an evil and troublesome gangster who has terrorised everybody in the village, because he is responsible for his father's death.

programme.title: Dhund
programme.desc: After crashing his car on a foggy night, a man seeks help at a nearby home, where he encounters a dead man and a woman holding a gun. After hearing her sad story, he helps her hide the crime.

programme.title: Justice Chaudhury
programme.desc: Justice Chaudhury is forced to go against his principles when criminal Shanker Singh's brother Jai Singh harasses his speech-impaired daughter Lakshmi.

programme.title: Karishmaa
programme.desc: Sunny, a fashion photographer, gets embroiled in an unnecessary controversy when two of his model friends are murdered. When his girlfriend is made the next target, he strives to save her.

programme.title: Teri Payal Mere Geet
programme.desc: Premi makes a living by selling poems to Benni and falls in love with a courtesan, Laila Jaan. However, he leaves her after finding the truth about her but tries to get back with her.

programme.title: Mr. X
programme.desc: Corrupt officials leave Officer Raghuram to die after he completes a secret operation. He consumes a healing solution with a side effect that makes him invisible and helps him take his revenge.

programme.title: Numbri Aadmi
programme.desc: Shankar, a noble thief, attempts to kill Rana, his father's murderer, in a bid to seek revenge. However, Rana has powerful friends who stand in Shankar's way.

programme.title: Zakhm
programme.desc: A boy, born into a family of varying religions, finds himself embroiled in communal riots. Soon, his mother's death becomes an issue raised to fulfil political agendas.

programme.title: Tum Haseen Main Jawaan
programme.desc: Sunil, a promiscuous navy officer, falls in love with Anuradha and introduces her to his family. However, an admirer of Anuradha tries to deceive Sunil by informing him that she is a single mother.

programme.title: Andaz 1971
programme.desc: This film tells the story of a widow Sheetal (Hema Malini) who is left devastated after the death of her husband Raj, (Rajesh Khanna). She is left with a son to look after and teaches in a school.

programme.title: Adalat
programme.desc: A farmer is aving 3 city boys from a tiger's theeth. In return they invite him to the city, where he find out that one of them rape his daughter.

programme.title: Bhrashtachar
programme.desc: A story of A journalist fighting corruption; A widow who becomes the mistress of local hoodlum Fatte Inspector Ashutosh, a political godfather who tries to win votes by promising the people a fair and an ideal government and Sattar.

programme.title: Andaz 1971
programme.desc: This film tells the story of a widow Sheetal (Hema Malini) who is left devastated after the death of her husband Raj, (Rajesh Khanna). She is left with a son to look after and teaches in a school.

programme.title: Dharmputra
programme.desc: During British rule in India, the families of Nawab Badruddin and Gulshan Rai share a close bond with each other. However, an unplanned pregnancy and a child born out of wedlock change this dynamic.

programme.title: Divorce
programme.desc: Mr. Oswal a rich, influential, and domineering man lives with his wife and two sons, namely Jayant and Ajay. He convinces his elder son, Jayant, that he cannot marry the girl he loves.

programme.title: Do Waqt Ki Roti
programme.desc: When a notorious criminal breaks out of prison, Jailer Vijay resigns and vows to arrest him. However, Shankar, a brother set on avenging his sister's death, stands in Vijay's way.

programme.desc: A family move in to their new house unaware of the fact that the house is inhabited by a ghost. It is learnt the ghost is not too happy with his new housemates.

programme.title: The Stoneman Murders
programme.desc: Mumbai of 1980s is terrorised by a serial killer who ruthlessly kills pavement dwellers in their sleep. A suspended police officer then decides to step in and conduct an investigation.

programme.title: Hum To Mohabbat Karega
programme.desc: A waiter, is in love with the famous TV reporter Greeta Kapoor. After a man is murdered, Kapoor shows up at Raju's door to ask him some questions - it turns out that Raju served the dead man his last supper.

programme.title: Kyo Kii... Main Jhuth Nahin Bolta
programme.desc: Raj is a lawyer who finds great success through his habit of lying. However, when his son wishes for his father to stop lying, Raj is magically forced to speak the truth and gets into trouble.

programme.desc: Jatin and Karishma, who meet each other on a plane to India, are set to marry the partners chosen by their respective parents. However, they find themselves attracted to each other.

programme.title: Sarkar

programme.title: Zameer: The Fire Within
programme.desc: Pooja gets married to Suraj but feels guilty for separating him from Supriya, his former fiancee. When Suraj learns the truth he abandons her, oblivious to the fact that she is pregnant.

programme.title: Kyaa Kool Hain Hum
programme.desc: Rahul is told by a street astrologer that he will find his soul-mate in a girl who has a birthmark on her right breast. Urmila Martodkar walks into his life, birthmark and all. But what they don’t know is that she is not a girl, but is a gay boy.

programme.title: Lucky: No Time for Love
programme.desc: The daughters of the Indian Ambassador in Russia, is being molested by a young Russian. She manages to escape and hide in a parked car, belongs to Aditya.

programme.title: Ghar Jamai
programme.desc: Dwarka, a millionaire, is impressed by Anil, a principled man, and wants him to marry Mona, his daughter. However, after their marriage, he compels him to move into his house.

programme.title: Hum To Mohabbat Karega

programme.title: Lal Baadshah
programme.desc: Lal Singh lives alongside his mother in a small basti in Mumbai, where Vikram Singh is the don of the city and there is always clash between him and Lal Baadshah.

programme.title: Namak
programme.desc: When Gopal, a servant, is announced as the heir to his employer's estate, the siblings of his master's family plot against him.

programme.desc: Shaun tries to ensure that Bitzer won’t be defeated in the box cart Grand Prix again.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Animated series. DM and Penfold go back to school to thwart the plans of an evil food scientist and his killer hot dogs.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: When a witch struggles to master her new wand, Twinkle decides to have a go, only to make her friends vanish. She must now go on a solo quest to find them.

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: When Dog reveals he's colour blind, the kids rally to help him see the world like they do. Mouse makes him some colour-correction glasses, and Dog is blown away by colour. He becomes an artist and neglects the family as he focuses on his painting career. But when the day of his art gallery premiere comes, Dog will have to choose between art and family.

programme.title: Newsround

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Gary has a pet translation collar.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck has to deliver pizzas around the town, but he hasn’t reckoned with the lengths that Frog will go to to get them all for himself.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck has a job punting tourists along the waterways, but a bored Frog thinks he can do a better job. Thanks to Frog's interference, the pair wind up going on a wild trip down the rapids.

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Odd Squad investigates strange events. A villain sets off a backwards bubble.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: It’s time for Gnasher’s annual bath, but the stinky hound will do anything to avoid the tub. Dennis and Gnasher go head to head in a town-wide game of cat and mouse – or rather, tripe hound and wheelie bathtub.

programme.title: Bitesize Daily: 5-7 Year Olds
programme.desc: Learn about meaningful maps, looking after the environment, and human and physical features.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Trying to solve the case of the mystery footprints, the brothers look at the animals’ feet. Could it be the penguin chicks, the snow leopard cubs, or something far more peculiar?

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Charles Darwin presents a special show, as Horrible Histories takes a timely look at our planet and how we’ve behaved towards it throughout history. We look at how we’ve not always treated the planet's resources with the utmost care, from the 19th-century gold rush, to Henry VIII enjoying a little too much deforestation to build his warships In The Gone Show, we meet a range of animals that have recently become extinct, and we see how mankind has tried to work with and shape the land, from the earliest guides to farming to the fabulous Gardens of Versailles In Historical Educating, head teacher Mrs Thomas has trouble with her geography supply teachers (a Greek philosopher and a Viking), who seem intent on telling their pupils that the Earth is not round. We reveal that plastic bags were originally invented to save the planet, and in Time Beefs, Judge Rinder settles a dispute between environmentalist Rachel Carson and the businessmen who tried to stop her.

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In this unprecedented time of lockdown there has never been a better time to learn about the wildlife around us, and Deadly 60 fans have been sending in their videos, photos and questions for Steve Backshall to answer. In this episode Steve focuses on our woodland wildlife, which hides some of our most fascinating and secretive animals. And judging by CBBC’s most observant viewers, our forests packed full of deadly animals! Steve explores why bees swarm, explains how bizarre bugs feed, and revels in the antics of a snuffling Badger! In a DIY demo, Steve then shows us how to catch Britain’s most secretive animals on film by using a camera trap! But that’s not all. The audience gets their chance to Beat Backshall in a 60 second quickfire round. The question is, how many can Steve get right before his time is up? In a fun-filled, fast and furious 13 minutes, we’ll also get to see some of the best bits from 13 years of deadly exploration. Steve gets stung by killer bees and gets a bit too close a south African Badger that’s armed with serious attitude!

programme.title: Art Ninja
programme.desc: Ricky crafts mini masterpieces, is set challenges by viewers, creates dynamic DIY animations and then gets out and about on his trusty skateboard to make epic art with a ninja twist! Gavin brings round his new pet, Spider, and Ricky is terrified. After making butterfly art inspired by Damien Hirst and weaving a ninja rug, Ricky has to run for his life when Spider escapes. Once in the woods, to try and overcome his fear, Ricky makes a sculpture with items he finds on the forest floor.

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Flying like a superhero may sound like enormous fun, but there are some serious downsides that Hollywood does not tell you about - weather, extreme cold and even fizzy blood.

programme.title: BBC Live Lessons
programme.desc: Join presenters Ade Adepitan and Megan McCubbin to learn about climate change. Find out what it is, why it’s happening and how to make everyday choices that help to reduce our carbon emissions. We’re going on a musical mission to put on a planet-friendly gig for band manager Hacker T Dog’s new signing, The Low-Carbon Legends. Along the way, we share a fun science experiment, make low-carbon event planning choices, and write song lyrics inspired by what we learn. Young gig producers across the UK share their climate-conscious ideas, and we hear from community gardener Tayshan Hayden-Smith and clothes designer Claudine Rousseau to discover how to help protect our planet in creative, collaborative and earth-kind ways. And, last but definitely not least, get ready to rock out during an outstanding unplugged performance from The Low-Carbon Legends.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Did you know your body is full of crystals? Herman the mantis shrimp is in the lab to help the doctors explain how your incredible body not only makes crystals in your bones and teeth but also stops them from doing damage to your kidneys. Say hello to new Oucher Anneeka, join Dr Dan as he goes on duty in a breathlessness clinic, and prepare for a huge medical discovery in Time Machine as the Cupboard of Everything heads into the past. Meanwhile, in the Emergency Department, Noha has arrived to find out why she keeps fainting, and has Jacob broken his toe?

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In this special episode of Deadly 60, Steve Backshall takes a deep dive into the fate of one of the most extraordinary, and endangered, ecosystems on our planet: coral reefs. They may cover less than one per cent of the ocean floor, but they support roughly a quarter of all marine life and are irreplaceable - yet they are in serious trouble. Steve travels to some of the world’s most celebrated reef systems, from the shallows of the Java Sea to the tropical waters of the Maldives. Along the way, he meets all the weird and wonderful creatures that call the reef home, including some of the best-camouflaged creatures and some impressive apex predators - he even gets a telling-off from a clownfish!

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Charles Darwin presents a special show, as Horrible Histories takes a timely look at our planet and how we’ve behaved towards it throughout history. We look at how we’ve not always treated the planet's resources with the utmost care, from the 19th-century gold rush, to Henry VIII enjoying a little too much deforestation to build his warships In The Gone Show, we meet a range of animals that have recently become extinct, and we see how mankind has tried to work with and shape the land, from the earliest guides to farming to the fabulous Gardens of Versailles In Historical Educating, head teacher Mrs Thomas has trouble with her geography supply teachers (a Greek philosopher and a Viking), who seem intent on telling their pupils that the Earth is not round. We reveal that plastic bags were originally invented to save the planet, and in Time Beefs, Judge Rinder settles a dispute between environmentalist Rachel Carson and the businessmen who tried to stop her.

programme.title: Georgia & Tommy: Baby Steps
programme.desc: Reality series following the TOWIE stars on their journey in parenthood and all the joy, tears, stress and magical moments that go with it. programme.title: The Only Way is Essex
programme.desc: It's the series finale, but the Essex drama shows no signs of packing up for the summer. Arg's behaviour pushes Gemma to breaking point. Jordan's friends give him the cold shoulder when he sticks to his principles. Myles gives Courtney an ultimatum. Dan struggles to commit to Amber while Chloe has doubts about her relationship with Adam. And Tommy is thrown a birthday party fit for a king.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Atlanta

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Limitless Win
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the quiz that gives contestants a chance to win the biggest cash prize in British TV history - every question is an opportunity to climb an endless money ladder and reach the big money, but only a correct answer banks the cash. programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Have you ever wondered how clever you are? This is your chance to find out as comedy legend Lee Mack presents the ultimate leveller of a quiz show - it doesn't matter if you're not usually good at quizzes because the questions have nothing to do with remembering facts and are all about logic and common sense. This is the quiz that the whole family can play together - 100 contestants begin every show and try to make it to the end to answer a question that only one per cent of the country can get right and win up to 100,000 pounds.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen
programme.desc: The American version of Gordon Ramsay's culinary boot camp. Teamwork gets tossed into the wok as the chefs face a high-pressure Chinese cuisine tag team challenge, but not everyone's ready to play nice - the heat then gets even hotter during a star-studded charity night dinner service benefiting Feeding America and Stand Up to Cancer. With each cook taking a turn running a course, the kitchen turns into a battlefield of egos, misfires, and shaky leadership.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. A proud Brian shows Stewie and Chris a hole he dug in the backyard where Rupert is revealed to be buried - while giving him a bath, Stewie discovers a Made in China tag on Rupert's back and realises that their relationship is multicultural. When Stewie suspects that Rupert wants him to meet his family for real, Stewie and Brian find the factory where Rupert was made but are mistaken for American spies.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. Peter signs up to take the place of the retiring Pawtucket Brewery's nightwatchman - when a drunk Principal Shepherd bribes him with cash to let him and his fellow principals steal some beer, Peter decides to turn the brewery into an after-hours bar. Meanwhile, Stewie and Brian find a lost dog poster with a big cash reward and exploit Brian's similarity to the missing pooch to try to claim the reward.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. Peter is sent out on a Father's Day scavenger hunt with Joe, Cleveland and Quagmire under the supervision of Principal Sheperd - after finding clues at Goodwill and Ernie the Giant Chicken's mansion, the hunt leads them to Wild West's ranch, where they find him preparing for a showdown with his absentee father Old West as he does every year - when Old West arrives to make amends, his son refuses to hear him out.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Animated comedy series. When Klaus gets sick, Stan baulks at paying the high cost to save his life. Klaus steals Stan's SUV and gets the CIA to transfer his brain into the SUV's navigation computer. Meanwhile, Steve and his friends are terrorised by Roger after they play a prank on one of his personas.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: US animated comedy series about the escapades of Stan Smith, an extreme right-wing CIA agent dealing with family life and keeping America safe in the most absurd way possible. When Hayley and Jeff fail to fall pregnant, Roger informs them that Jeff is an alien with only a human brain and that they won't be able to conceive unless an alien gives birth to a fully-human Jeff.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: The iconic TV series is back, but in a brand-new setting with brand-new prize boxes and a new batch of contestants hoping to win a whopping cash prize - who will have what it takes to beat our brand-new Banker? Now hosted by Stephen Mulhern, the series will see contestants battle it out against the notorious Banker to be in with a chance of winning a life-changing cash prize in a nail-biting game of nerves and intuition. Contestants are confronted with 22 sealed boxes with each one hiding a different amount of money - without knowing what each one contains, the contestant must eliminate the boxes, which are opened one at a time, and the amount of money inside is revealed. Pressure mounts as the player is tempted by the notorious Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for what might be inside their box, and as long as the larger cash prizes remain unchosen, the Banker's offers will get higher... but that can all change in an instant if a big cash prize is unveiled.

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Graham Norton hosts The Neighbourhood, a brand-new high stakes, street-sized popularity contest. Set in the heart of The Peak District, six households from across the UK move into a 'picture-perfect' village to compete for a massive £250,000 prize. But don't let the ducklings and the quaint cafe fool you - behind the smiles and the sandwiches, the knives are already out. The households live side by side 24/7 and try to keep their neighbours' onside to avoid being voted out during 'a removal'. They have a chance to save themselves from the removals by winning immunity in the neighbourhood challenges. Not wasting any time, Graham suspends the neighbours seven metres high in the first immunity challenge that gets them all in a spin, with revelations about each other they'd perhaps rather not air in public. Which household can snatch safety and save themselves from the first vote? And who is going to find themselves in the firing line early on?

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. Meg is enjoying her new life with Ivan in Russia, but she still misses some aspects of her old one - when the pair then have a near-death experience with the country's dissident laws made out by Vladimir Putin, Ivan proposes to her as he does not want to risk losing her again. Meanwhile, Chris and Principal Shepherd decide to go into the adult industry in order to allow Chris to earn his extracurricular credits.

programme.title: New: I'm a Celebrity... Unpacked Live

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Anarchic animated sitcom about a dysfunctional family in a wacky Rhode Island town. When Meg shoves past the other students at lunch and gets asked to try out for the Adam West High basketball team as a result, it soon becomes clear that Peter's belittling is what motivates her to play at her best - meanwhile, Stewie discovers several baby-centric conspiracy videos on YouTube - including one that tells him the real reason behind nap time - and begins to fall further down the rabbit hole.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Lloyd begins to think Finlay is his son after Teresa reveals they shared a night of passion years ago. A besotted Becky pushes Jason too far and Chesney wins Kayleigh's affections.

programme.desc: Teresa leads Lloyd on a guilt trip, prompting him to contemplate shelling out cash to support Finlay. Vernon is unsettled by Liz's attentiveness and Becky drives Jason to despair.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Lewis and Hathaway investigate the murder of a professor of German history who had a fascination with local underground boxing bouts - when they learn that one of his students was the latest winner of one fight, they suspect that the murder may have connections to the underworld.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Police drama series set in Yorkshire in the 1960s. Nick investigates graffiti at a battery egg farm and discovers that there is more to the case than he thought. Meanwhile, Greengrass tries his hand at selling free range eggs.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Police drama series set in Yorkshire in the 1960s. The apparently simple case of a lost suitcase reveals a long-standing family feud, and Nick becomes involved in a security alert at the village fete.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Alan King, co-owner of King’s Crystal with his brother Charles, is killed in a car accident in Shanghai during a business trip with his brother Charles and accountant Peter Baxter - the company faces bankruptcy and the workforce, which is led by volatile glassblower Jack Tewson, is devastated. Tensions are brewing throughout Midsomer Magna - as Alan’s son Ian shuns his mother Hilary to grieve with his close friend David Monroe, Sophie Baxter, the daughter of Peter, is in love with Ian while undertaker James Mason is in love with Sophie. Meanwhile, Cully is rehearsing for a production of Hamlet at the Midsomer Open Air Theatre which has been refurbished thanks to Alan’s fundraising - as Barnaby watches Cully in the performance of Hamlet, he realises that the play contains a message sent by Ian King before he died. Can the detective pick up the trail of clues to lead him to the killer?

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Daz and Jake take one of the Kings' vans for a joyride. Debbie worries about money when Carl demands overdue mortgage payments, and Jimmy arranges a date over the Internet.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Debbie can't pay her mortgage, so asks her family to repay the money she lent them for Belle's school fees. Sam is elated when Jo hugs him, and Jimmy complains about his love life.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: A brooding Jason confides in Eileen that things with Becky are moving too fast for him. Trouble brews between Steve and Dan, while Gail prepares for Ted's visit and David's return.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Dan's feud escalates and an row breaks out on the street. Becky tries to keep Jason keen by splashing out on sexy underwear, and Gail and Tina bring David home.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Police drama series set in Yorkshire in the 1960s. Thomas Abbott has received an offer to sell his farm to a neighbouring farmer when a Land Rover is purposefully backed into the Abbotts' wall and causes a sheep exodus - meanwhile, Kate is called out to visit a 10-year-old girl and is puzzled by her symptoms, Greengrass tells Gina that an Australian has offered an award in the local press for locating his missing twin brother and enlists her help, and the police uncover some illegal exotic bird dealing and suspect that it may be linked to strange illnesses within the area.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Police drama series set in Yorkshire in the 1960s. Kate and Nick meet young Danny Parkin, an eight-year-old with tuberculosis whose family want to send him to a Swiss sanatorium but do not have enough money - meanwhile, Kate recruits Greengrass to be Father Christmas at a village charity concert, and Nick investigates a seasonal crime involving stolen Christmas trees.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While family life is far from normal at the home of Eileen Fountain - the steward at prestigious Whiteoaks Golf Club - club captain Martin Crisp is trying to buy back nearby Pellings, a rambling manor house once owned by his family, from golfing president Will Tunstall. As the peaceful setting of the prestigious club is rocked by death and controversy when a golfer is bludgeoned to death near the notorious 13th hole known as Crisp's Folly, Barnaby and Jones learn that crime is rife there...

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Detective drama series based on novels by Peter Robinson. DCI Banks is called in to investigate a fire - evidence suggests that the victim was involved in a lucrative art forgery scam, but confusion mounts as to the killer's intended victim when a second body is discovered. Banks becomes convinced that one of the victims' relatives is withholding vital information, but his theory brings him into conflict with DS Annie Cabbot - is Cabbot herself crossing the line when she begins an affair with the art expert on the case?

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Detective drama series based on novels by Peter Robinson. When Tom McMahon dies in a second suspicious fire, Banks focuses his investigation on the ringleader of the art forgery scam - when he discovers that Annie has started a relationship with Mark Keane, the art expert in the investigation, Banks worries that their actions will seriously compromise the case. Determined to prove a connection between the Asperns and the murders, Banks realises who the serial killer might be - and that Annie's life is now in grave danger.

When Mimi drops dead, could a missing band member hold the key... or will Barnaby find that one of his heroes is a murderer?

programme.desc: Andy surprises Jo by arriving home early from his break and when Sam turns up for lunch she tells him they have to cancel. programme.desc: Dan turns up at the Rovers to sort things out with Steve but a fight develops, leaving the bookie badly injured. Gail falls out with Audrey, and Becky seduces a powerless Jason.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama series based on characters created by Ann Cleeves. DCI Vera Stanhope is called to a remote lighthouse when the body of a man is discovered in a sailboat - despite the difficulties of the scene, Vera immediately sets about her investigation. Vera learns that the body is local council enforcement officer and experienced amateur sailor Frank Channing, a family man with strong friendships and who was well respected at work... surely this really was just an accident? As Vera looks into Frank's life, she finds that he perhaps wasn't the diligent and dedicated worker everyone said he was - and that maybe his facade of being a happy-go-lucky family man had started to crack. It's starting to look like Frank was also seriously out of his depth and messing with things that many people would rather he left alone - had Frank started to pick at things that caused murder to unravel? Vera and her team piece together Frank's final days and soon discover that his life wasn't plain sailing.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Detective drama series based on novels by Peter Robinson. When Annie looks into the murder of a paralysed and disfigured woman whose body is found on the moor, her first case as senior investigating officer proves challenging - when the identity of the victim becomes apparent, Annie establishes that any number of people might have had reason to want her dead. Meanwhile, Banks investigates when a teenage girl's body is found in an alleyway in Eastvale - how did the killer get there without being seen?

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Detective drama series based on novels by Peter Robinson. Meanwhile, Annie is convinced that there is a connection between the deaths of Templeton and Lucy Payne, but her ideas fall on deaf ears and - unbeknownst to the team - there is a traitor in their midst.

When one of Renton's employees is found murdered, the police realise she must have been killed because she knew something about Thornton's death - as Lewis finds that Thornton, Renton and Leland were all at Oxford together 20 years before, he realises that this is actually an age-old revenge story...

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: David Mostyn is shocked to find his mother Delyth in bed with Dr James Kirkwood, a senior partner in the Midsomer Market surgery, but the beautiful divorcee is delighted when James uses the occasion to propose to her - her daughter Megan, James' practice manager, is equally outraged and especially when Delyth tells her they are planning to sell up and travel the world. James also has the difficult task of telling Dorothy Hutton, his late wife's mother who is still grieving, and asks his surgery partner Alan Delaney to check on Dorothy while lending him his brand-new Range Rover for the journey - when Alan has to leave the car during a storm, a pick-up truck drives into him at speed and kills him. Barnaby and Jones dig into Alan's past and discover that James once accused him of stealing 30,000 pounds from the practice, but it was put down to clerical error and the two doctors made up - as Barnaby and Jones investigate, they find that they must travel to Snowdonia to unravel the truth.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jo admits to Sam that Andy beats her but refuses to leave her husband. Daz takes Victoria to the van to see the stash, and the Kings continue their hunt for the thief.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series. The departure of Dr Dibbs sees Martin move back into his old surgery and agree to stay for a couple of months until a replacement can be appointed - determined to get to grips with fatherhood, he asks Louisa to move into his house so he can help to raise their child and even suggests that Louisa and the baby could move to London with him. Louisa reluctantly agrees to move into the Doc's house and offers to help with reception duties as Pauline has left, but she is none too sure about the move to London as she had hoped to resume her career as headmistress of the village school - meanwhile, Portwenn packs out the village church for the funeral of the Doc's Aunt Joan. As the villagers are shocked when Martin's eulogy becomes a health warning about the dangers to the heart of eating the wrong food, Joan's sister Ruth attends the funeral and demonstrates the same cantankerous character as her nephew after learning that she is to inherit Joan's house and farm.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series. Sleepless nights take their toll on Martin and Louisa as they try to find ways to comfort their constantly wailing baby, and Aunt Ruth deals with an intruder on the farm who has stolen the hubcaps from her car - when the thief is identified as Michael Dunwich, who lives with his mother Shirley, Ruth decides to pay a visit to the mother and her son. It soon becomes clear to psychiatrist Ruth that Michael is a bit odd as he has a collection of metal objects strewn around the garden - meanwhile, Shirley makes an appointment with Martin to discuss crippling stomach pains that she has been suffering from. When her son is out of the room, Shirley tells the Doc that she thinks that her son is trying to poison her so he can have the house to himself - elsewhere, new receptionist Morwenna Newcross fails to make a good impression by arriving late on her first day, appearing to be hyperactive, and getting sacked when Martin alleges that she is high on drugs.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Detective drama series based on novels by Peter Robinson. DCI Banks is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find Rydell's missing daughter - with only an internet lead to go on, can Banks find Emily and persuade her to come home? Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the thief is found brutally executed in his own home - DS Annie Cabbot struggles to identify credible suspects, but she and Banks come to a devastating realisation when another body is found.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part story. With the discovery of a body in the nightclub, Banks finds himself at the heart of a murder investigation with any number of suspects and many unanswered questions - when a surveillance operation establishes a clear link between Barry Clough and the murder of Charles McKay, Banks and Cabbot realise that the two cases must be connected. The investigation then takes a number of alarming twists which push the detectives' relationship to breaking point and reveal a shocking secret within their own team.

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and his partners Allen Millyard and Guy Willison are back on the road trawling Britain's sheds and auction houses for rusty relics to restore and sell on for a tidy profit. This time they head down to Dorset to meet Tim, the owner of a delectable collection of trinkets, and come away with some choice items to bulk up their stock - they then return to the Ledbury auctions for yet more items before heading down the road to the Cotswold Oil Engine and Preservation Society's famous bring and buy sale.

programme.title: A View to a Kill
programme.desc: The 14th entry in the James Bond film series and the last to star Roger Moore as the secret agent. When James Bond is sent to investigate a security leak at high-tech company Zorin Industries, he discovers a hotbed of murder and deception - Max Zorin, the company's mysterious owner has devised a plan to corner the world's microchip market... even if he has to kill millions to do it. Before Bond can stop Zorin, he must confront the madman's beautiful and deadly companion May Day - with help from geologist Stacey Sutton, Bond launches an all-out assault on Zorin's deadly scheme that concludes with a treacherous duel on the upper spans of the Golden Gate Bridge.
